channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Ted Turner, CNN Founder and Media Pioneer, Dies at 87
item.description: Ted Turner, the visionary founder of CNN, passed away at age 87. Born in Cincinnati, Ohio, in 1938, Turner attended a military boarding school and briefly studied at Brown University before being expe...

item.title: Deputy President Mashatile Conducts Oversight Visit to Address Service Delivery in North West
item.description: Deputy President Paul Mashatile is scheduled to visit the Ditsobotla Local Municipality (DLM) in the North West on Thursday to assess progress in improving service delivery. The visit follows concerns...

item.title: Key Updates on U.S. Primaries and Political Dynamics Ahead of Midterms
item.description: As the U.S. primary season intensifies, voters are determining which candidates will compete in the November midterm elections. In Ohio, Vivek Ramaswamy, formerly a presidential candidate known for hi...

item.title: Bushra Bibi moved to Rawalpindi Eye Hospital for routine post-surgery follow-up
item.description: Bushra Bibi, spouse of former Prime Minister and PTI founder Imran Khan, was shifted from Adiala Jail to Al-Shifa Trust Eye Hospital in Rawalpindi for a scheduled follow-up examination of her right ey...

item.title: Karla Hiraldo Voleau Uses Photography to Process Betrayal and Reclaim Personal Narrative
item.description: Photographer Karla Hiraldo Voleau, originally from the Dominican Republic and based in Lausanne, Switzerland, created the project “Another Love Story” as a way to process emotional trauma following a ...

item.title: Coroner Rules Girl's Death on Rope Swing Accidental
item.description: A 12-year-old girl, Brooke Wiggins, tragically died in November 2024 after a tree branch fell on her while she played on a rope swing in Banstead, Surrey. The coroner, Ivor Collett, concluded that her...

item.title: Beatrice Chebet Celebrates Upcoming Motherhood with Family and Friends
item.description: Kenyan long-distance running star Beatrice Chebet recently celebrated a major milestone as she prepares to become a mother. Friends, family, and fellow athletes hosted a lavish baby shower in her hono...

item.title: Family mourns 25-year-old following South London shooting
item.description: A 25-year-old man tragically lost his life in a shooting incident in South London, prompting his family and local community to pay tribute to him. Although specific details of the circumstances surrou...

item.title: Ted Turner's Vision Made the Atlanta Braves a National Baseball Icon
item.description: When Ted Turner purchased the Atlanta Braves in 1976, the team was struggling both on the field and in attendance. Rather than focusing solely on baseball performance, Turner recognized the Braves as ...

item.title: Nigerian Reps Demand Urgent Action Over Attacks on Citizens in South Africa
item.description: The Nigerian House of Representatives don raise alarm as new wave of xenophobic attacks dey target Nigerians wey dey South Africa. Dem call on the Federal Government make e act sharply, using diplomat...

item.title: Ex-Head of Service, Oyo-Ita, Cleared of N570m Alleged Fraud After Six-Year Trial
item.description: The Federal High Court for Abuja don finally discharge and acquit former Head of Service of the Federation, Winifred Oyo-Ita, over the N570 million money laundering wahala wey the EFCC bring against h...

item.title: Ruai Family Seeks Missing Sister Who Left Son Alone
item.description: A family in Ruai, Nairobi, is urgently searching for their sister and mother, Regina Wamuyu, who mysteriously went missing from her home, leaving her young son behind with a note. According to her bro...

item.title: Ghana High Commission Advises Nationals to Exercise Caution Amid Johannesburg Protest
item.description: The Ghana High Commission in South Africa has issued an official safety advisory for Ghanaian nationals following a protest in the Johannesburg Central Business District (CBD) on 29 April. The protest...

item.title: Hantavirus outbreak on cruise ship near Cape Verde raises health concerns but low public risk
item.description: One cruise ship carrying 149 people is currently stranded near Cape Verde after a hantavirus outbreak led to the death of three passengers and left others seriously ill. Authorities refused the ship p...

item.title: Woman killed by partner before gas explosion at their London home
item.description: A tragic incident occurred in Stoke Newington, east London, in June 2025, when 46-year-old Annabel Rook was fatally attacked by her partner, 45-year-old Clifton George. According to court reports, the...

item.title: Amazon Launches Contest for U.S. Residents to Submit Alien or UFO Footage
item.description: Amazon-owned Ring has announced a contest inviting U.S. residents to capture evidence of extraterrestrial life using their Ring devices. Participants can submit videos featuring aliens or UFOs, but en...

item.title: Del Monte Kenya’s Economic Impact Surpasses Sh100 Billion Over Two Decades
item.description: Del Monte Kenya has made a significant contribution to the country’s economy, exceeding Sh100 billion over a 20-year period between 2004 and 2024. According to an impact report prepared by Lotus Consu...

item.title: PTI confirms Punjab Coordination Committee decision remains unchanged
item.description: Islamabad – PTI Secretary General Salman Akram Raja on Wednesday clarified that the party would not discuss the formation of the Punjab Coordination and Monitoring Committee at the moment, as leadersh...

item.title: Robert Irwin recalls spilling orange juice during lunch with King Charles
item.description: Robert Irwin, the young Australian wildlife conservationist and son of the late Steve Irwin, shared a humorous yet mortifying experience from his youth while appearing on The Tonight Show with Jimmy F...

item.title: Concerns Raised Over Treatment of Jewish Candidate in Ontario Medical Association Election
item.description: Hal Berman, a Jewish palliative care physician in Toronto, alleges that he was singled out during the Ontario Medical Association (OMA) presidential election due to his social media posts addressing a...
